Name: Oddish
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move. It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor. The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Bellossom
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 80 damage for each heads. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Plentiful in the tropics. When it dances, its petals rub together and make a pleasant ringing sound.

Name: Tangela
Flavor-Text: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tangrowth has two arms that it can extend as it pleases. Recent research has shown that these arms are, in fact, bundles of vines.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As Scyther fights more and more battles, its scythes become sharper and sharper. With a single slice, Scyther can fell a massive tree.

Name: Sunkern
Flavor-Text: It lives by drinking only dewdrops from under the leaves of plants. It is said that it eats nothing else.

Name: Yanma
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

Name: Yanmega
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

Name: Kricketot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When its antennae hit each other, it sounds like the music of a xylophone.

Name: Cherubi
Flavor-Text: The deeper a Cherubi’s red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Leafeon V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Leafeon VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grubbin
Effects: Attach a [L] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

Name: Zarude
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

Name: Calyrex
Effects: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.

Name: Charizard V
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard VSTAR
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Radiant Charizard
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] less for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Combustion Blast.
Flavor-Text: It spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. It may cause forest fires by blowing flames.

Name: Entei
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Simisear V
Effects: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Simisear VSTAR
Effects: You may discard up to 5 cards from the top of your deck. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
This attack does 30 damage for each Energy card in your discard pile. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: The people of ancient times believed that Larvesta fell from the sun.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Volcarona scatters burning scales. Some say it does this to start fires. Others say it’s trying to rescue those that suffer in the cold.

Name: Volcanion
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

Name: Salandit
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its venom sacs produce a fluid that this Pokémon then heats up with the flame in its tail. This process creates Salandit’s poisonous gas.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Only female Salazzle exist. They emit a gas laden with pheromones to captivate male Salandit.

Name: Seel
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its thick fat, cold seas don’t bother it at all, but it gets tired pretty easily in warm waters.

Name: Galarian Mr. Mime
Effects: Search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It can radiate chilliness from the bottoms of itsnfeet. It’ll spend the whole day tap-dancing on anfrozen floor.

Name: Wailmer
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It shows off by spraying jets of seawater from the nostrils above its eyes. It eats a solid ton of Wishiwashi every day.

Name: Wailord
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can sometimes knock out opponents with the shock created by breaching and crashing its big body onto the water.

Name: Corphish
Flavor-Text: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

Name: Snorunt
Flavor-Text: It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as -150 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Luvdisc
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.
Flavor-Text: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola’s branches.

Name: Kyogre
Effects: Search your deck for a [W] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand. This attack does 180 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Name: Kyogre V
Effects: This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Aqua Typhoon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Glaceon V
Effects: Search your deck for a [W] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Shinx
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

Name: Luxio
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxio
Effects: This attack does 90 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has a Pokémon Tool attached. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio’s electricity and power up its own electric blasts.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Luxray’s ability to see through objects comes in handy when it’s scouting for danger.

Name: Luxray
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in your hand when you are setting up to play, you may put it face down as your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

Name: Rotom V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rotom VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may use this Ability. Discard any number of cards from your hand. Then, draw that many cards. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: Put any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile in the Lost Zone. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you put in the Lost Zone in this way.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: As it flies, it scatters electricity around, so bird Pokémon keep their distance. That’s why Emolga can keep all its food to itself.

Name: Eelektrik
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

Name: Helioptile
Flavor-Text: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon’s electric moves.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.

Name: Radiant Charjabug
Ability-Effect: Whenever any player attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon V, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

Name: Zeraora
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It approaches its enemies at the speed of lightning, then tears them limb from limb with its sharp claws.

Name: Zeraora V
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zeraora VMAX
Effects: This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Zeraora VSTAR
Effects: You may discard a Stadium in play.

Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon 4 times. (You can choose the same Pokémon more than once.) For each time you chose a Pokémon, do 60 damage to it. This damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pincurchin
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: Attach up to 2 [P] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
If your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Mew V
Effects: Search your deck for an Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Fusion Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Girafarig
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent by biting.

Name: Lunatone
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

Name: Dusclops
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

Name: Tapu Lele
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Although called a guardian deity, Tapu Lele is devoid of guilt about its cruel disposition and can be described as nature incarnate.

Name: Hatterene V
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck. You may attach any number of Energy cards you find there to this Pokémon. Put the other cards back in any order.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hatterene VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move up to 2 damage counters from your Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Enamorus
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winterncomes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon’s lovengives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

Name: Graveler
Flavor-Text: It travels by rolling down cliffs. If it falls into a river, it will explode with its last gasp.

Name: Solrock
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Lunatone.
Flavor-Text: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

Name: Baltoy
Flavor-Text: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: It’s exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

Name: Pancham
Flavor-Text: It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master’s actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: This attack does 80 less damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This form of Lycanroc is reckless. It chargesnheadlong at its opponents, attacking without anyncare about what injuries it might receive.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.

Name: Absol
Effects: Put a random card from your opponent’s hand in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

Name: Liepard
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Krokorok
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It buries some of its prey in the sand to use as emergency meals when its hunts are unsuccessful.

Name: Pangoro
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.

Name: Skrelp
Flavor-Text: It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead.

Name: Dragalge
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If this Pokémon evolved from Skrelp during this turn, put 8 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1 during Pokémon Checkup.
Flavor-Text: Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea.

Name: Hoopa
Effects: If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

Name: Galarian Meowth
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Living with a savage, seafaring people hasntoughened this Pokémon’s body so much thatnparts of it have turned to iron.

Name: Galarian Perrserker
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

Name: Scizor
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Bulky pincers account for one third of Scizor’s body weight. A single swing of one of these pincers will crush a boulder completely.

Name: Aron
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.

Name: Lairon
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Lairon live in mountains brimming with spring water and iron ore, so these Pokémon often came into conflict with humans in the past.

Name: Aggron
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.

Name: Metang
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Two Beldum have become stuck together via their own magnetic forces. With two brains, the resulting Metang has doubled psychic powers.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: It uses river stones to maintain the cutting edges of the blades covering its body. These sharpened blades allow it to bring down opponents.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It’s accompanied by a large retinue of Pawniard. Bisharp keeps a keen eye on its minions, ensuring none of them even think of double-crossing it.

Name: Zacian
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancientntimes, this Pokémon needs only a single strikento fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.

Name: Zacian V
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 160 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zacian VSTAR
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zamazenta
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it beingnknown as the Fighting Master’s Shield.nIt was feared and respected by all.

Name: Zamazenta V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 5 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zamazenta VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may use this Ability. During your opponent’s next turn, all of your Pokémon take 100 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (This includes Pokémon that come into play during this turn or during your opponent’s next turn.) (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza V
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
You may discard up to 2 basic [R] Energy or up to 2 basic [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.
Effects: You may discard any amount of basic [R] Energy or any amount of basic [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 3 cards.
Effects: You may discard any amount of basic [R] Energy or any amount of basic [L] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Duraludon V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Duraludon VMAX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that have Special Energy attached.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Radiant Eternatus
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon VMAX and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Flavor-Text: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

Name: Tauros
Effects: If this Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: When Tauros begins whipping itself with its tails, it’s a warning that the Pokémon is about to charge with astounding speed.

Name: Ditto
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Basic Pokémon in your discard pile, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Flavor-Text: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

Name: Eevee V
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Snorlax
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

Name: Starly
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Bidoof
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Regigigas V
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regigigas VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, if your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. They discard that Pokémon and all attached cards. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: Shuffle up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

Name: Stoutland V
Effects: If your opponent’s Basic Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 1 more Prize card.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Yungoos
Flavor-Text: Although it will eat anything, it prefers fresh living things, so it marches down streets in search of prey.

Name: Gumshoos
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon moved from the Bench to the Active Spot during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Patient by nature, this Pokémon loses control of itself and pounces when it spots its favorite meal—Rattata!

Name: Oranguru
Effects: Choose a Supporter card from your opponent’s discard pile and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.
Flavor-Text: With waves of its fan—made from leaves and its own fur—Oranguru skillfully gives instructions to other Pokémon.

Name: Greedent V
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Draw 3 cards.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wooloo
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

Name: Dubwool
Effects: This attack also does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

Name: Bea
Description: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck, and attach any Energy cards you discarded in this way to your Benched [F] Pokémon in any way you like.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Bede
Description: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Digging Duo
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, look at the bottom 8 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. If tails, look at the bottom 3 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

VSTAR Power
The Pokémon V this card is attached to can use the VSTAR Power on this card.

Name: Switch
Description: Switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Ultra Ball

Name: Elesa’s Sparkle

Name: Friends in Hisui
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Friends in Sinnoh
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Volo
Description: Discard 1 of your Benched Pokémon V and all attached cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

Name: Hisuian Voltorb
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only during your first turn. Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to anPoké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it hasnstored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.

Name: Kricketune
Ability-Effect: Your [G] Pokémon in play, except any Kricketune, get +40 HP. You can’t apply more than 1 Swelling Tune Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: It signals its emotions with its melodies. Scientists are studying these melodic patterns.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: All of your Fusion Strike Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t apply more than 1 Lesson in Zeal Ability at a time.
Effects: Put 5 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

Name: Lapras
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon during their last turn, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

Name: Manaphy
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

Name: Electivire
Effects: If any of your Benched Magmortar have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.
This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

Name: Toxtricity
Ability-Effect: If all your Pokémon in play are Fusion Strike Pokémon, your opponent’s Pokémon VMAX in play get -30 HP.
Flavor-Text: It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with what sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.

Name: Mew
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck, reveal an Item card you find there, and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its DNA is said to contain the genetic codes of all Pokémon, so it can use all kinds of techniques.

Name: Lunatone
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The phase of the moon apparently has some effect on its power. It’s active on the night of a full moon.

Name: Deoxys
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Fusion Strike Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: DNA from a space virus mutated and became a Pokémon. It appears where auroras are seen.

Name: Diancie
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Basic Pokémon.
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

Name: Comfey
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Put the other card in the Lost Zone.
Flavor-Text: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won’t wither once they’re attached to a Comfey.

Name: Solrock
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Lunatone.
Flavor-Text: When it rotates itself, it gives off light similar to the sun, thus blinding its foes.

Name: Absol
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.

Name: Thievul
Ability-Effect: If your opponent has 2 or fewer Prize cards remaining, whenever your opponent plays a Supporter card from their hand, prevent all effects of that card done to your Benched Pokémon V.
Flavor-Text: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Magnezone
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of [M] Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Some say that Magnezone receives signals from space via the antenna on its head and that it’s being controlled by some mysterious being.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, shuffle your deck, then put that card on top of it.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Latias
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Latios.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Pokémon VMAX.
Flavor-Text: It can telepathically communicate with people. It changes its appearance using its down that refracts light.

Name: Hisuian Goodra
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to each of your Basic Pokémon that has any [M] Energy attached by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V.
Flavor-Text: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy— it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

Name: Ditto
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can use the attacks of any Basic Pokémon in your discard pile, except for Pokémon with a Rule Box (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes). (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)
Flavor-Text: It can reconstitute its entire cellular structure to change into what it sees, but it returns to normal when it relaxes.

Name: Dunsparce
Ability-Effect: [C] → Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Weakness :
Flavor-Text: When it sees a person, it digs a hole with its tail to make its escape. If you happen to find one, consider yourself lucky.

Name: Miltank
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Miltank produces highly nutritious milk, so it’s been supporting the lives of people and other Pokémon since ancient times.

Name: Bibarel
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: It’s exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

Name: Swablu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If it finds bad children who won’t listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it’s said.

Name: Bidoof
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text:
It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle
down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

Name: Pikachu
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Pikachu that can generate powerful electricity have cheek sacs that are extra soft and super stretchy.

Name: Turtwig
Flavor-Text: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

Name: Paras
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The mushrooms, known as tochukaso, are controlling the bug. Even if the bug bugs the mushrooms, they tell it to bug off.

Name: Poochyena
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has a very tenacious nature. Its acute sense of smell lets it chase a chosen prey without ever losing track.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You’ll want to pet it because it’s cute, but if you use your bare hand, you’ll get a painful shock.

Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: Put 3 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regigigas VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, if your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. They discard that Pokémon and all attached cards. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)

Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.

Name: Adaman
Description:
You can use this card only if you discard 2 [M] Energy cards from your hand.
Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cheren’s Care

Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Grant
Description:
During this turn, your [F] Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Irida
Description: Search your deck for a [W] Pokémon and an Item card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

Name: Raihan
Description:
You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn.
Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Roxanne
Description:
You can use this card only if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prize cards remaining.
Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 6 cards, and your opponent draws 2 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Origin Forme Palkia VSTAR

Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.

VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Arceus VSTAR
